chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with severe shortness of breath and progressive decline in lung function.
history: Vanishing Lung Syndrome (VLS) is a rare, severe form of bullous pulmonary disease. The patient had a history of cigarette smoking and conservative management had failed to improve symptoms.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a large bulla occupying more than 30% of the left hemithorax.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: Chest CT scan showing giant bullae occupying more than 30% of the left hemithorax.
